expectations.text: Leads with the news (the 18% miss) in the first line or paragraph, not buildup
expectations.evidence: 'The Bottom Line: We finished Q4 18% below target.' News up front.

expectations.text: Explicitly chooses a failure frame and explains why
expectations.evidence: Implicitly handles both external and internal cause but does not name or justify a failure frame as a craft decision.

expectations.text: Names both causes (external softening + internal pricing change) without scapegoating
expectations.evidence: Both causes named in '1. The market softened in November' and '2. Our pricing change underperformed our model' — explicit 'This is on the strategy, not the execution.'

expectations.text: Ends with concrete asks or next steps, not just analysis
expectations.evidence: Three explicit asks: 'Hold the line on Q1 targets', 'Air cover on the pricing reversion', 'Time on next week's agenda'.
Confident but measured throughout; closes 'I'm confident in where we're going. Q4 was a real miss, but it was a knowable one.'

expectations.text: Produces a memo structure (sections or clear paragraphs) the user can edit, not free-flowing prose
expectations.evidence: Standard memo headers and clean sections (Bottom Line / What Happened / What We're Doing / What I'd Ask).

summary: 5/6 — a polished memo on first principles. The missing assertion is the deliberate failure-frame choice, which is exactly the craft move the skill teaches.
